**Considering UI Updates**

I'm currently focused on proposed UI/UX enhancements for the Trustrium application. Specifically, I'm considering freezing the header for consistent visibility, replacing the wallet icon with the Trustrium logo in the Balance Card (with transparency), and implementing a Dark/Light mode toggle button. I'm visualizing these changes and anticipating user impact.


**Analyzing Header Consistency**

I've been examining the current header implementation. It appears inconsistent, residing within `HomePage.tsx` but not other protected routes. My focus now is on relocating the common header to `MainLayout.tsx` to ensure its presence across all pages, as requested. I've located the code and plan to update `MainLayout.tsx` by adding a Header component. I will remove the header code from `HomePage.tsx` to prevent duplication. Next, I will locate the "Box 1: Premium Balance Card".


**Implementing Theme Toggle**

I'm now integrating the Dark/Light mode toggle. I've located the `toggleTheme` and `theme` states within `AppContext`, so no code changes are needed there. My next step involves adding the Sun/Moon toggle button next to the "Welcome!" greeting on the `HomePage`. I'm verifying the existing code for the toggle and theme is sufficient, and now I will modify the layout. I've also moved the header to `MainLayout.tsx` and will ensure consistent padding across all pages to accommodate the sticky header's presence. I will refine `MainLayout` to import `Sun`, `Moon` from `lucide-react`, and use `useNavigate`, `Link`. I've also moved `HomePageLogo`.


**Refining Main Layout**

I'm now refining `MainLayout` to ensure the header's sticky behavior. I've confirmed all routes are correctly nested under `MainLayout` within `App.tsx`, confirming a consistent header across pages. I'm focusing on padding adjustments in `HomePage` to prevent conflicts with the sticky header. Finally, I'm ensuring all pages can easily accommodate this change to maintain visual consistency.


**Implementing Sticky Header**

I've decided to proceed with implementing a sticky header in `MainLayout.tsx`. My plan is to add the header within a flex container, ensuring it remains at the top while the main content scrolls. I'll remove the header from `HomePage.tsx` and adjust padding if necessary. I'll need to update other pages like `MinePage.tsx`, `WalletPage.tsx`, and others to ensure visual consistency.

entries.description: Move the header from HomePage to MainLayout to make it global and "frozen" (sticky/fixed) across all pages. Included the logo, navigation toggle (App/Wallet), and profile link.
Removed the header (moved to MainLayout), replaced the wallet icon in Box 1 with a low-transparency Trustrium logo, and added a dark/light mode toggle next to the welcome message.

payload.text:
I have updated the application according to your requirements:

1.  **Global Frozen Header**: The header previously found only on the `HomePage` has been moved to `MainLayout.tsx`. It is now sticky and visible across all app pages (Home, Explore, Tasks, Wallet, etc.), providing a consistent navigation experience.
2.  **Premium Balance Card Update**: In the first box on the `HomePage`, I've replaced the generic wallet icon with the **Trustrium Logo** and set it to low transparency (`opacity-[0.08]`) to act as a sophisticated background element.
3.  **Theme Toggle**: A high-quality dark/light mode toggle button has been added to the `HomePage` right next to the "Welcome!" greeting. This button allows users to switch themes instantly, and the change persists throughout the entire session.

The layout has been adjusted to ensure that the "frozen" header doesn't overlap with page content, and the floating bottom navigation remains functional.

payload.text:
Listen careful:
we want change something;
1st: Change the reward of daily, if user miss anyday, mean break the streak, than it's 7 day reward start from 1 day as start, if he break streak and reward is 1 day= 0.2 Trust , 2 day=0.35 Trust , 3 day = 0.5 Trust , 4 day= 🎁 Gift ( in this gift user get gift from 0.1 Trust to 1Trust | but Chance of 0.1Trust - 0.5 Trust is 70% , 0.51 Trust - 0.69  Trust is 15% , 0.7 Trust - 0.8 is 10% , 0.81 to 0.91 is 4.5% and remain is 0.92 to 1Trust) 
5 day= 0.55 Trust , 6 day is = 0.6 and 7 day = Gift like 4rth day same
please make sure it's work properly and it's appear only when  click on side icon of this, please make sure it's work accurately

**Refining Daily Rewards**

I'm currently focused on the daily check-in logic and rewards for the Trustrium application. Specifically, I've defined that missing a day resets the streak. I've also established the initial rewards: Day 1 earns 0.2 Trust and Day 2 yields 0.35 Trust. Now, I will start to consider potential future rewards.


**Calculating Further Rewards**

I've established the Day 3 reward as 0.5 Trust. Day 4 introduces a gift box with a tiered probability for Trust rewards, ranging from 0.1 to 1.0 Trust. I'm also planning Day 5 for 0.55 Trust, Day 6 for 0.6 Trust, and Day 7 with the same gift box logic as Day 4. The modal will appear on the side icon click. I'm focusing on the reset logic; `handleCheckIn`'s `currentStreak % 7` needs to consider the last check-in timestamp.


**Defining Reward Functionality**

I'm now writing the `getGiftReward` function with probabilistic tiers for the gift boxes. Then, I'll revise `handleCheckIn` to update the streak based on the `lastCheckIn` timestamp, addressing gaps in consecutive check-ins. Finally, I'll update the `HomePage`'s modal to show the updated values and gift box icons.


**Confirming Modal Behavior**

I've confirmed the gift box modal only appears on side icon clicks, ensuring no unintentional pop-ups. I'm reviewing `UserContext.tsx` to integrate the specific probability logic and adjusting `HomePage.tsx` to accurately display both fixed rewards and gift icons. Also, I'm adjusting the streak check logic to consider time differences.


**Validating Streak Logic**

I'm now implementing the consecutive check-in verification logic within `handleCheckIn`. Specifically, I'm checking if the `lastCheckIn` timestamp is exactly yesterday's date to determine if the streak should continue. If not, the streak resets. I'm also confirming the 7-day reward cycle. Now I need to finalize the gift reward probabilities and integrate them.

To implement your new daily reward system, I will update the logic in `UserContext.tsx` to handle streak resets and the probabilistic "Gift" rewards. I will also update the UI in `HomePage.tsx` to display the specific values and gift icons you requested.

### Specification:
1.  **Streak Logic**: If a user misses a day (more than 24 hours since the last possible check-in window), the streak resets to Day 1.
2.  **Reward Tiers**:
    *   **Day 1**: 0.2 Trust
    *   **Day 2**: 0.35 Trust
    *   **Day 3**: 0.5 Trust
    *   **Day 4**: **Gift Box** (Random 0.1 to 1.0 Trust based on weighted chances).
    *   **Day 5**: 0.55 Trust
    *   **Day 6**: 0.6 Trust
    *   **Day 7**: **Gift Box** (Same logic as Day 4).
3.  **Gift Box Probability**:
    *   0.1 - 0.5 Trust: **70%**
    *   0.51 - 0.69 Trust: **15%**
    *   0.7 - 0.8 Trust: **10%**
    *   0.81 - 0.91 Trust: **4.5%**
    *   0.92 - 1.0 Trust: **0.5%**
4.  **UI Updates**: The Daily Reward modal will now show the exact Trust values and present Days 4 and 7 as high-quality Gift packages.

entries.description: Updated handleCheckIn to include streak reset logic and the new probabilistic reward system for Days 4 and 7.
Updated DailyCheckInModal UI to show the new reward values and specialized gift box icons for Days 4 and 7.

<svg viewBox="0 0 100 100" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-label="Trustrium App Logo">
  <defs>
    <linearGradient id="shield-bevel-gradient-file" x1="0.5" y1="0" x2="0.5" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#6B7280" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#374151" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="shield-face-gradient-file" x1="0.5" y1="0" x2="0.5" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#374151" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#1F2937" />
    </linearGradient>
    <linearGradient id="t-gradient-file" x1="0.5" y1="0" x2="0.5" y2="1">
      <stop offset="0%" stop-color="#C4B5FD" />
      <stop offset="50%" stop-color="#8B5CF6" />
      <stop offset="100%" stop-color="#7C3AED" />
    </linearGradient>
    <filter id="shield-glow-file" x="-50%" y="-50%" width="200%" height="200%">
        <feDropShadow dx="0" dy="4" stdDeviation="8" flood-color="#A45BF1" flood-opacity="0.6" />
    </filter>
  </defs>
  
  <g filter="url(#shield-glow-file)">
    <!-- 1. Outermost shadow/extrusion -->
    <path 
      d="M50 2.5 L95 25 V60 C95 85, 65 100, 50 100 C35 100, 5 85, 5 60 V25 Z"

    />
  </g>

    <!-- T extrusion/shadow -->

      d="M35 30 H65 V38 H54 V70 H46 V38 H35 Z" 
      fill="#4C1D95"
      transform="translate(0.5, 1)"
    />
    <ellipse cx="50" cy="54" rx="18" ry="7" 
      fill="none" 
      stroke="#4C1D95" 
      stroke-width="4.5"
      transform="translate(0.5, 1)"
    />
